project_timeline.buffer_reason: SaaS domain with multiple third-party integrations (QuickBooks, Slack, Google Calendar, Figma, GitHub). 10% buffer per domain rules for SaaS. SOC2 compliance overhead is partially absorbed within sprint capacity planning rather than additional buffer since security controls are built-in from Sprint 1.

team_composition.rationale: VERY_HIGH complexity (48 FRs, 11 modules, SOC2 compliance) demands 12+ team members. 14-person team provides sufficient parallelism across backend, frontend, and QA streams to meet the aggressive 5-month timeline. Two UI/UX designers are needed because the platform has both internal dashboards and a client-facing portal requiring distinct UX paradigms. DevOps engineer is critical for CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code, and SOC2 compliance tooling.

goals: Establish development environment, CI/CD pipeline, and infrastructure-as-code
Finalize solution architecture and technology stack decisions
Set up project scaffolding with multi-tenant database schema design
Configure monitoring, logging, and SOC2-compliant audit trail infrastructure
deliverables: Development environment with Docker Compose for local development
CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions) with linting, testing, and deployment stages
Infrastructure-as-code (Terraform) for staging environment
Database schema design document and initial migration scripts
Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) for technology choices
SOC2 control mapping document

team_focus.backend: Set up Node.js/TypeScript project scaffolding, configure PostgreSQL with multi-tenant schema, implement database migration framework, set up API framework (Express/Fastify)
team_focus.frontend: Set up React/TypeScript project with design system foundation, configure Storybook for component library, establish responsive layout framework
team_focus.qa: Set up test frameworks (Jest, Playwright), configure test automation pipeline, define QA process and acceptance criteria templates, establish test environments

goals: Implement RBAC with all 7 predefined roles and granular permissions
Build 2FA enrollment and login flow (TOTP + SMS fallback)
Implement password policies and session management
Build admin panel foundation for user and role management
Establish audit trail logging infrastructure
deliverables: User registration, login, and authentication API with JWT tokens
RBAC engine with 7 roles and permission matrix
2FA enrollment flow (TOTP and SMS)
Password policy enforcement (12-char minimum, complexity, expiry, reuse prevention)
Session timeout with 30-minute inactivity and 5-minute warning
Audit trail logging for all authentication events
Admin panel: user CRUD, role assignment, user invitation workflow

team_focus.backend: Implement authentication service (JWT + refresh tokens), RBAC middleware, 2FA service (TOTP via speakeasy, SMS via Twilio), password policy engine, session management, audit trail event system with immutable log storage
team_focus.frontend: Build login page, 2FA setup wizard, password change flow, session timeout modal, admin panel layout with user management screens and invitation workflow
team_focus.qa: Write security test cases for authentication flows, RBAC permission matrix validation, 2FA bypass attempt tests, session timeout verification, audit log completeness checks

goals: Build centralized project dashboard with filtering and sorting
Implement project CRUD with configurable fields
Build task management with status workflows and dependencies
Implement milestone tracking with target dates and deliverable associations
deliverables: Project dashboard showing all active projects with status, team, deadlines, completion %
Project creation/edit/archive functionality with all configurable fields
Task management: creation, assignment, status transitions (To Do, In Progress, In Review, Done), dependencies
Milestone definition with target dates, deliverable links, and client approval flags
Project-level RBAC enforcement (only assigned team members see project internals)

team_focus.backend: Build Project and Task domain models, REST APIs for CRUD operations, task dependency graph logic, milestone service with approval state machine, tenant-scoped queries
team_focus.frontend: Build project dashboard with data tables (filtering, sorting, pagination), project creation wizard, task list view with inline editing, milestone timeline component
team_focus.qa: End-to-end tests for project lifecycle, task workflow transitions, dependency validation, milestone approval flows, RBAC enforcement on project resources

goals: Build Kanban board view with drag-and-drop task management
Implement time tracking with start/stop timer and manual entry
Build configurable billing rates (global, client, project levels)
Implement time entry approval workflow
deliverables: Kanban board view with drag-and-drop between status columns
Time tracking: start/stop timer, manual entry, billable/non-billable flag
Configurable billing rates with hierarchical override (global < client < project)
Time entry approval workflow (submit, approve, reject, request modification)
Timesheet views: weekly and monthly summaries with project breakdown

team_focus.backend: Build time entry service with timer state management, billing rate hierarchy engine, approval workflow state machine, timesheet aggregation queries with billable/non-billable breakdowns
team_focus.frontend: Implement Kanban board with react-beautiful-dnd or similar, timer widget (persistent across navigation), manual time entry form, approval queue view, timesheet grid with weekly/monthly toggle and export
team_focus.qa: Test Kanban drag-and-drop across browsers, timer accuracy and persistence tests, billing rate override precedence validation, approval workflow edge cases, timesheet calculation accuracy

goals: Build client-facing portal with project dashboard and data isolation
Implement deliverable review and milestone approval through portal
Build portal messaging/communication feature
Implement document management with upload, versioning, and preview
deliverables: Client portal login with strict data isolation (tenant-filtered queries)
Client project dashboard showing status, milestones, and recent activity
Deliverable review: view, download, approve, or request revision with comments
Threaded messaging between client and project team
Document repository with folder structure, upload/download/preview/delete
Automatic version history with view, compare, and restore capabilities

team_focus.backend: Build client portal API layer with tenant isolation middleware, deliverable approval state machine, messaging service with WebSocket for real-time updates, document storage service (S3-compatible) with versioning engine
team_focus.frontend: Build client portal UI shell (separate theme/layout from internal app), client dashboard, deliverable review interface, threaded messaging component, document manager with folder tree, upload progress, and in-browser preview (PDF, images, Office docs)
team_focus.qa: Client data isolation penetration tests (cross-tenant access attempts), deliverable approval workflow tests, messaging delivery and notification tests, document upload/version/restore tests across file types

goals: Build client portal timeline/Gantt visualization
Implement automated client email notifications
Build document commenting and annotation
Implement client approval workflow for deliverables
Build visual resource allocation board

Automated email notifications for milestone completion, deliverable uploads, approvals, messages
Document commenting with threaded replies and notifications
Deliverable submission and client approval workflow (Submit > Review > Approve/Reject/Request Changes)
Visual resource allocation board showing team members, assignments, and utilization %

team_focus.backend: Build email notification service (SendGrid/SES) with event-driven triggers and user preferences, document comment service, deliverable approval workflow engine, resource allocation query service with utilization calculations
team_focus.frontend: Implement Gantt chart component (using dhtmlxGantt or custom D3.js), email notification preferences UI, document annotation/commenting overlay, deliverable approval UI flow, resource allocation board with utilization bars and availability indicators
team_focus.qa: Test email notification delivery and content for all event types, document commenting and annotation across browsers, deliverable approval state transitions, resource utilization calculation accuracy

goals: Complete resource assignment with conflict detection
Build capacity forecasting (4-12 week lookahead)
Implement QuickBooks integration with bi-directional invoice sync
Build QuickBooks connection configuration interface
Implement Slack notification integration
deliverables: Resource assignment and reassignment with overallocation conflict detection
Capacity forecasting view (4-12 weeks) filtered by team, skill, department
QuickBooks OAuth connection and configuration admin panel
Bi-directional invoice sync: platform to QuickBooks (finalized invoices) and QuickBooks to platform (payment status)
Slack integration: configurable event notifications to channels/DMs

team_focus.backend: Build resource assignment service with capacity threshold conflict detection, forecasting engine using current schedule data, QuickBooks OAuth2 flow and API client (invoice push, payment status pull), sync queue with retry logic, Slack webhook/bot integration with event routing
team_focus.frontend: Resource drag-and-drop assignment UI with overallocation warnings, capacity forecast chart (filterable by team/skill/department), QuickBooks admin configuration panel (OAuth, account mapping, sync logs), Slack integration settings page

Build Figma and GitHub integrations

team_focus.backend: Build Google Calendar API integration (OAuth, event CRUD, auto-sync on date changes), Figma oEmbed/API for thumbnail generation, GitHub API client for commit/branch/PR data, invoice generation engine (aggregate approved time entries, apply billing rates, generate draft), executive dashboard aggregation queries, SSO service (SAML 2.0/OIDC with Google Workspace)

sprints.sprint_name: Reporting, Analytics, Platform Features & Data Migration
goals: Build project profitability reports with variance analysis
Implement configurable report export (PDF, CSV, Excel) with scheduling
Build NPS tracking and trend display
Implement responsive web application cross-browser compatibility
Build global search and notification center
Implement data migration import wizard
deliverables: Project profitability reports: budget vs actual, variance at project/client/portfolio levels
Report export in PDF, CSV, Excel formats with scheduled email delivery
NPS score recording, trend charts, and executive dashboard integration
Responsive web app tested across Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge at 768px-1920px
Global search across projects, tasks, documents, clients (RBAC-filtered)
In-app notification center with real-time updates, categories, and preferences
Data migration wizard: CSV/Excel upload, validation, dry-run preview, batch import

team_focus.backend: Build report generation engine with variance calculations, PDF/CSV/Excel export service (using puppeteer/pdfkit for PDF, exceljs for Excel), scheduled report cron jobs with email delivery, NPS data model and aggregation, Elasticsearch/PostgreSQL full-text search index, WebSocket notification service, CSV/Excel parser with validation engine and batch import transaction management
team_focus.frontend: Report builder UI with parameter selection and chart visualizations, export buttons and schedule configuration dialog, NPS entry form and trend chart, responsive layout audit and fixes across breakpoints, global search bar with autocomplete and result categories, notification bell with dropdown panel and preference settings, data migration wizard with file upload, field mapping, validation results, and import progress
team_focus.qa: Report calculation accuracy against raw data, export format validation (PDF rendering, CSV/Excel data integrity), scheduled report delivery tests, NPS trend chart data accuracy, cross-browser responsive testing matrix, search relevance and RBAC filtering tests, notification delivery timing and preference enforcement, data migration with valid/invalid/edge-case data files

goals: Stub out Phase 2 features (mobile app API, AI estimation, advanced analytics) for future development
Comprehensive end-to-end integration testing across all modules
Performance testing and optimization (page loads, API response times, dashboard queries)
Security hardening: penetration testing remediation, OWASP Top 10 validation
SOC2 compliance verification and documentation
deliverables: Mobile-optimized API endpoints documented for Phase 2 native app development
AI estimation feature flagged and stubbed with historical data collection hooks
Advanced analytics data pipeline foundations for Phase 2
Full integration test suite passing across all 11 modules
Performance benchmarks: pages < 2s, dashboards < 5s, APIs < 500ms (95th percentile)
Security scan report with zero critical/high findings
SOC2 control evidence documentation package

team_focus.backend: API optimization (query tuning, caching strategy with Redis, connection pooling), security hardening (input validation audit, rate limiting on auth endpoints, CSRF tokens), SOC2 evidence collection (encryption verification, access control audit, backup procedures), Phase 2 API endpoint stubs
team_focus.frontend: UI performance optimization (code splitting, lazy loading, image optimization), accessibility audit and fixes, cross-browser regression testing, error message review for user-friendliness
team_focus.qa: End-to-end integration test execution across all modules, load testing (200 concurrent users), penetration testing coordination and finding validation, SOC2 control testing, regression test full suite execution

goals: Execute User Acceptance Testing with TechNova stakeholders
Fix UAT-identified defects
Complete user documentation and training materials
Prepare production deployment runbook
Execute data migration dry-run with TechNova's actual data
Go-live readiness review
deliverables: UAT test execution report with pass/fail/blocked status
All critical and high UAT defects resolved
User guide for internal users and separate client portal user guide
Administrator operations manual
Production deployment runbook with rollback procedures
Data migration validation report from dry-run with real data
Go-live checklist signed off by all stakeholders
Production environment provisioned and configured

team_focus.backend: UAT defect fixes (priority-driven), production environment configuration, database migration scripts for production, data migration execution and validation, deployment automation scripts, monitoring and alerting setup (CloudWatch/Datadog)
team_focus.frontend: UAT defect fixes (UI/UX issues), user guide screenshots and content, client portal onboarding flow polish, final responsive design adjustments
team_focus.qa: UAT facilitation and defect triage, regression testing after UAT fixes, data migration validation (record counts, data integrity checks), production smoke test planning, go-live readiness checklist verification

milestones.name: Architecture Sign-Off
milestones.description: Solution architecture, technology stack, database schema design, and infrastructure plan reviewed and approved by technical and client stakeholders. ADRs documented.

milestones.name: Core Module Demo — Auth & Project Management
milestones.description: Demo of working authentication system with RBAC and 2FA, admin panel, and project management dashboard with task and milestone tracking. First tangible product demo to the client.

milestones.name: Client Portal Alpha Release
milestones.description: Client portal with project dashboard, deliverable review, messaging, document management, and resource allocation board demo to TechNova. Client users can be invited for early feedback.

milestones.name: Feature Complete — All Integrations Live
milestones.description: All 48 functional requirements implemented. QuickBooks, Slack, Google Calendar, Figma, GitHub integrations operational. Executive dashboard and reporting available. SSO enabled.

milestones.name: Performance & Security Certification
milestones.description: Performance benchmarks met (pages < 2s, APIs < 500ms). Security scan shows zero critical/high vulnerabilities. SOC2 control evidence documented and reviewed. System ready for UAT.

milestones.name: UAT Start
milestones.description: User Acceptance Testing begins with TechNova stakeholders. Dedicated UAT environment provisioned with migrated test data. UAT test scripts provided to client team.

milestones.name: Go-Live Readiness
milestones.description: All UAT defects resolved, production environment ready, data migration validated, deployment runbook approved, go-live checklist complete. Final sign-off for production deployment.

parallel_streams.team_members: 4 Backend Developers (1 Senior/Tech Lead, 2 Mid-Level, 1 Junior) + DevOps Engineer for infrastructure
parallel_streams.description: Continuous backend development from authentication through all modules. Backend leads frontend by 1-2 days within each sprint to unblock frontend integration. Tech Lead owns API design and code review.

Frontend development starts with component library and design system in Sprint 0-1, then builds feature UIs in lockstep with backend APIs. Senior Frontend Dev owns the component library and responsive framework.

Design leads development by 1 sprint. Sprint 0: design system and core layouts. Sprint 1: auth and admin wireframes. Sprint 2-3: project management and time tracking. Sprint 4-5: client portal (distinct UX). Sprint 6-7: integrations, reporting dashboards, and data migration wizard. Designs are reviewed with BA before handoff to frontend.

QA is active throughout the entire project. Sprint 0: test framework setup. Sprints 1-8: test case creation and execution in parallel with development (testing features from the current and previous sprint). Sprint 9: dedicated integration testing, performance testing, security testing. Sprint 10: UAT support and regression.

parallel_streams.team_members: 1 Senior Backend Developer + 1 Mid-Level Backend Developer (focused allocation)
parallel_streams.description: QuickBooks, Slack, Google Calendar, Figma, and GitHub integrations are concentrated in Sprints 6-7. The Senior Backend Dev leads QuickBooks (most complex, bi-directional sync), while the Mid-Level Dev handles Slack, Calendar, Figma, and GitHub integrations in parallel.

Sprint 0: CI/CD pipeline, Docker, Terraform, staging environment. Sprint 1: monitoring and logging infrastructure. Sprints 2-7: maintenance mode (pipeline updates as needed). Sprint 8-9: performance optimization, production environment setup. Sprint 10: deployment automation and go-live support.

parallel_streams.team_members: Backend Tech Lead + QA Lead + External SOC2 Consultant (part-time)
parallel_streams.description: Sprint 0: SOC2 control mapping and security architecture. Sprint 1: core security implementation (RBAC, 2FA, audit trails). Ongoing: security controls built into each sprint's development. Sprint 9: dedicated security hardening, penetration testing, SOC2 evidence collection.

parallel_streams.team_members: 1 Backend Developer + 1 QA Engineer + BA (client coordination)
parallel_streams.description: Sprint 8: build migration wizard and import engine. Sprint 10: dry-run with TechNova's actual data, validation, and production migration execution. BA coordinates with TechNova for data cleansing and field mapping.

sprint_1.focus: Authentication, RBAC, 2FA, audit trails, admin panel, user management

sprint_3.focus: Kanban board, time tracking, billing rates, approvals, timesheets

sprint_4.focus: Client portal, deliverable review, messaging, document management, versioning

sprint_5.focus: Gantt view, email notifications, document comments, approval workflow, resource board

sprint_6.focus: Resource assignment, capacity forecasting, QuickBooks, Slack

sprint_7.focus: Google Calendar, Figma, GitHub, invoicing, executive dashboard, SSO

sprint_8.focus: Profitability reports, exports, NPS, responsive web, search, notifications, data migration

sprint_9.focus: Phase 2 stubs (mobile, AI, analytics), integration testing, performance, security

sprint_10.focus: UAT execution, defect fixes, documentation, deployment preparation

dependency_map.description: Key inter-module dependencies that drive sprint ordering

dependencies.reason: All modules depend on RBAC, authentication, and audit trail infrastructure being in place first
Admin user management and invitation workflow must exist before client portal users can be onboarded or data migration can import user records
Project management core (projects, tasks, milestones) must exist before Kanban views, client portal project display, time tracking against tasks, and resource allocation against projects
Time entries must be trackable and approvable with billing rates before invoices can be generated and synced to QuickBooks
Document repository and versioning must exist before commenting/annotation and client approval workflows can be built on top of documents
QuickBooks sync configuration depends on the invoice sync feature being implemented
Executive dashboard and profitability reports depend on time tracking, timesheet, project, and resource data being available for aggregation

risk_mitigations_in_plan.mitigation_in_plan: QuickBooks integration spike can begin during Sprint 3 (BA research) with full implementation in Sprint 6. Bi-directional sync queue with retry logic is a dedicated deliverable.

risk_mitigations_in_plan.risk: R-002 — Data migration from unstructured spreadsheets
risk_mitigations_in_plan.mitigation_in_plan: BA requests sample data in Sprint 1. Migration wizard built in Sprint 8 with validation engine. Dry-run with real data in Sprint 10. Two iterations of migration testing planned.
11 sprints across 22 weeks (+ 1 week buffer = 23 weeks / ~5.3 months). 14-person team enables parallel streams. Client demos every 2 sprints. Strict scope control via SOW.
Security controls built from Sprint 0-1 (not retrofitted). Audit trails, RBAC, encryption, and session management are Sprint 1 deliverables. Sprint 9 dedicated to SOC2 evidence collection.

Phase 2 features (FR-043, FR-044, FR-045) are LOW priority and stubbed/flagged in Sprint 9 rather than fully built. If budget pressure arises, MEDIUM priority items (FR-015, FR-022, FR-032, FR-038, FR-039) can be descoped to Phase 2.

capacity_notes.sprint_velocity_assumptions: Each 2-week sprint assumes 8 story points per developer and 5 story points per QA engineer. With 7 developers and 2 QA engineers, theoretical sprint capacity is 66 story points. Practical capacity is ~50 points (75% efficiency accounting for meetings, code review, and unplanned work).
capacity_notes.highest_load_sprints: Sprints 7 and 8 carry the highest feature load (6-7 FRs each) but this is achievable because: (1) many are integration features with well-defined API contracts, (2) the team is at peak productivity by this point, and (3) UI/UX design is complete by Sprint 7 so frontend has no design dependencies.
capacity_notes.buffer_allocation: The 10% buffer (~2.2 weeks) is distributed as: 1 week absorbed into Sprint 9 hardening (which has only 3 LOW-priority stub FRs, leaving capacity for overflow), and 1 week added to the end date (August 21 vs. a pure 22-week end of August 7).
